topIssues: Excessive use of borders and nested containers creates visual noise and violates the Chrome Collapse principle.
The large bottom example cards compete directly with the primary search input for the user's attention.
Too many secondary actions (Upload, Paste, Voice, Camera) are visible simultaneously, cluttering the main input area.
Awkward horizontal alignment and crowding between 'Suggested' prompts and 'LENS' filters.
Inconsistent padding within the 'More starting points' card, specifically the 'CR' avatar being too close to the edge.
recommendations: Remove the borders from the 'Suggested' and 'LENS' pills, using subtle background fills on hover instead to reduce visual noise.
Collapse the 'Upload, Paste, Voice, Camera' buttons into a single '+' attachment menu or borderless icon row inside the input box.
Remove the large bordered containers around the bottom sections ('Start with one example' and 'More starting points') and rely on typography for section headers.
Consolidate the bottom example cards into a simpler, text-based list to ensure the search input is the undisputed dominant job.
Separate the 'Suggested' and 'LENS' sections into distinct vertical blocks rather than cramming them onto the same horizontal plane.

topIssues: Extreme visual clutter from excessive borders, violating the Chrome Collapse principle.
Empty state shows pre-allocated answer boxes (What it is, Why it matters) before a question is asked, confusing the user.
Sidebars (Context, Sources, Agent Activity) compete with the primary chat input for attention on initial load.
Too many secondary actions (Go deeper, Show risks, Save report, Open full report) are visible before any content is generated.
Dashed borders for empty states feel unpolished and add to the wireframe-like visual noise.
recommendations: Center the chat input vertically and horizontally on the initial load (like ChatGPT/Google) to create a single, undeniable primary action.
Remove all pre-allocated answer boxes ('What it is', etc.) from the empty state; only render them dynamically once generation begins.
Hide the right sidebar (Sources, Agent Activity) completely until the user submits a prompt.
Remove 1px borders from internal panels and use subtle background fills or whitespace to establish hierarchy.
Consolidate the left sidebar context pills into a single 'Context' dropdown or inline indicator attached to the chat input.

topIssues: Excessive use of borders and bounding boxes (Chrome Collapse failure) creates severe visual clutter across the entire screen.
Split attention between the middle column (cards) and right column (details) makes it hard to know where to focus.
The middle 'Starter' cards are overloaded with text, tiny columns, and neon bars, making them hard to scan.
The top header section takes up too much vertical space for static onboarding text, pushing primary content down.
The 'Memory Loop' instruction card in the bottom left competes with the actual navigation and breaks the visual pattern.
recommendations: Remove the outer borders from the main layout panels (Header, Left Nav, Middle Section, Right Sidebar) and use whitespace to separate them.
Simplify the middle report cards: show only Title, Subtitle, and a single metadata tag, removing the internal 'WHAT IT IS / STARTER' columns.
Consolidate the right sidebar into a single continuous scrolling pane with typographic headers instead of 4 separate bordered boxes.
Shrink the top header 'Reusable memory' section to a simple H1 + subtitle without a bounding box to push primary content higher up.
Move the 'Save one useful run' onboarding text into an empty state in the middle column rather than a permanent left-nav fixture.

topIssues: Excessive use of borders and containers (Chrome Collapse failure) makes the UI feel heavy, cluttered, and outdated.
Meta-commentary and onboarding text ('Do not build a broad notifications center first', 'Next Action Loop' steps) permanently occupy valuable screen real estate.
Competing primary actions: Multiple 'Open in Chat' buttons in the feed compete with the massive 'Open in Chat' button in the right sidebar.
The right sidebar acts as a static explainer rather than providing dynamic, contextual information related to a selected nudge.
The header section is unnecessarily enclosed in a massive bordered box, disconnecting it visually from the feed below.
recommendations: Remove all nested borders around cards and containers; use whitespace, background color shifts, and typography to establish hierarchy.
Delete the meta-commentary card ('Do not build...') and the static 'Next Action Loop' explainer to focus entirely on the user's actual nudges.
Consolidate the primary action: Make the entire nudge card clickable, or keep a single clear CTA per card, and remove the redundant sidebar CTA.
Remove the bounding box around the top header ('A closed loop...') to let it breathe and integrate naturally with the page background.
Move 'Connected Channels' to the 'Me' (settings) surface to keep the Nudges feed strictly focused on actionable alerts.

topIssues: Excessive use of borders (chrome) creates visual clutter and makes the interface feel heavy and boxed-in.
Lack of a single dominant job; the user is presented with onboarding tasks, profile settings, and stats simultaneously without clear prioritization.
High cognitive load due to dense, paragraph-length text blocks in the left and middle columns.
The primary CTA ('Start in Chat') is buried at the bottom of the left column instead of being a clear focal point.
Inconsistent visual treatment of similar data (e.g., stats pills in hero vs. stats list in right column).
recommendations: Remove all inner borders from the cards in the three columns and use whitespace/padding to separate the content.
Consolidate the 'What improves the next run' text blocks into a single, scannable checklist or progress indicator.
Move the 'Start in Chat' CTA to the hero section to establish a clear primary action for the screen.
Convert the paragraph-length descriptions in the 'Profile' column into concise, editable fields or bullet points.
Standardize the display of metrics by removing the bordered pills in the hero section and integrating them into the 'Context already here' column.
